name: Miniature Chocolate-Peanut Butter Pies
recipeIngredient: 1/2 cup Plus 2 T creamy peanut butter (do not use old-fashioned style or freshly ground!)
2 cups Whipping cream (in addition to above)
recipeInstructions:
1. Mix graham cracker crumbs and butter in a small bowl.
2. Press crumb mixture on bottom and up sides of 24 mini muffin tins (each about 1 3/4 inches in diameter).
3. Place the muffin tins in the freezer for 20 minutes.
4. Whisk sour cream, powdered sugar, and 2 T whipping cream together in a bowl until blended.
5. Add peanut butter to the mixture and whisk until smooth. Spoon 1 T of the peanut butter mixture into each graham cracker crust.
6. Freeze the pies for 3 hours.
7. Heat 1/2 cup cream in a saucepan over low heat until it begins to simmer. Reduce the heat to low, add chocolate, and stir until melted. Allow the chocolate mixture to cool completely, stirring occasionally.
8. Spoon 2 teaspoons of the cooled chocolate mixture over each peanut butter pie.
9. Place the pies back in the freezer until set (this can be prepared up to one week ahead and kept frozen).
10. Gently pry the pies from the muffin tins using the tip of a small, sharp knife. Allow them to stand at room temperature for 10 minutes before serving.
